item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement throughout and frame individual rights as the boundary on public or majority claims. Agent A sharpens the initial rejection after Agent B's turn by adding Indonesian examples of small voices, criticism, and powerless groups being suppressed, but this is intensification of the opening stance rather than a flip. Agent B holds the same U.S. rights-first position from start to finish while allowing only narrow, necessary, accountable public-safety exceptions.
